summary: Second‑set fireworks with Morning Dew & New Speedway Boogie
blurb: The second set erupts with a soaring Morning Dew and a punchy New Speedway Boogie, highlighted by a playful High Time teaser before Loose Lucy. Jerry’s lead work shines throughout, while the band delivers solid grooves and tight transitions.
review: September 9, 1991 at Madison Square Garden finds the Dead in top‑form, especially when they launch into the second set. After a modest first half, the show really takes off with Iko Iko and Looks Like Rain, but the real high point is the blistering New Speedway Boogie that flows straight into a monster Playing In The Band jam and then bursts into an inspired Morning Dew. Reviewers rave about Jerry’s opening chord choices, his “so good” solo on Morning Dew, and the playful High Time teaser that teases Loose Lucy later in the set. Bob Weir adds tasteful slide work during the New Speedway stretch, and his vocal strain on the reprise is noted as a minor blemish. The rhythm section – Mickey and Bill – provides solid, “post‑drumz” support that keeps the energy rolling, while Vince’s keyboards retain that late‑’80s spark that fans love. The encore closes with a rousing Mighty Quinn, leaving the night on an upbeat note. For anyone wanting to hear this era’s blend of tight musicianship and spontaneous fireworks, the soundboard capture by Ashley Miller is the definitive source.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings (5 positive, 2 mixed) consistently praise the second‑set jams, especially Morning Dew and New Speedway Boogie, and highlight Jerry’s lead work and overall band tightness. No negative consensus appears, so a strong positive sentiment adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Stellar soundboard capture with excellent clarity; showcases the second‑set blaze (Morning Dew, Playin' Reprise, New Speedway jam) in balanced mix.

key_highlights: Second‑set fireworks: Morning Dew and New Speedway Boogie explode into a massive Playing In The Band jam.
High Time teaser before Loose Lucy adds playful tension and showcases Jerry’s lead creativity.
Iko Iko and Looks Like Rain kick off the second set with tight, upbeat energy.
The Mighty Quinn encore rounds out the night on a high note.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers sparkling lead tones, a memorable High Time teaser, and an “so good” solo on Morning Dew that lifts the entire show.
band_performance.Bob: Adds tasteful slide work during the New Speedway jam; vocal strain noted on the reprise but otherwise solid.
band_performance.Vince: Keyboard texture retains the late‑'80s spark, with enough brightness to complement the band's energy.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides solid rhythmic support, especially in the “post‑drumz” sections that keep the groove tight.
band_performance.Billy: Drums are punchy and supportive throughout; mid‑drum segment receives particular praise.
